text: An implementation for the analytic solution to the multi-target Bayes recursion known as the δ-Generalized Labeled Multi-Bernoulli ( δ-GLMB) filter proposed by Vo and Vo in [“Labeled Random Finite Sets and Multi-Object Conjugate Priors,” IEEE Trans. Signal Process., vol. 61, no. 13, pp. 3460-3475, 2014]. This paper focuses on the implementation details of the proposed δ-GLMB multi-target tracking filter. This filter iterates between update and prediction stages, and both stages involve computation of a weighted sum of multi-target exponentials that have an unmanageable number of terms. We exploit the structure of the δ-GLMB distribution and utilize techniques derived from ranked assignment and K-th shortest path algorithms in the update stage and prediction stage to obtain a sub-set of the terms with the highest values, in order to obtain a manageable number of terms for computations. We also derive cheap look-ahead schemes from our implementation of the probability hypothesis density (PHD) filter in the same framework. Finally, we give a characterization of the L1-error in multi-target density due to truncation.
explanation: The text uses precise, field-specific terminology (δ-GLMB, PHD, ranked assignment, K-th shortest path) and cites a real IEEE paper, which strongly suggests it is human-written or excerpted from literature rather than randomly generated; its structure resembles a research abstract.
